commands.name: initial static characterization before fix
commands.result: Failed acceptance: output found legacy implementation in layout.tsx, including useAuth, useListingDetail, TABS, /dashboard/listings hrefs, text-sand, border-gold, text-gold, and material-symbols-outlined. Page modules already contained redirect stubs.

commands.name: static scan of old route files for redirect targets and absence of legacy UI implementation
commands.result: Passed. Redirect target matches were found for page.tsx, knowledge/page.tsx, logistics/page.tsx, offers/page.tsx, and spa/page.tsx. The inverted legacy implementation scan returned no matches.

commands.name: script verification command
commands.result: Passed. Static Next route module verification status=pass with no failures; final_url_map recorded below.

commands.name: TypeScript compiler
commands.result: Passed with no stdout or stderr.

commands.name: changed file pure LOC check
commands.command: for f in frontend/src/app/'(app)'/dashboard/listings/'[id]'/layout.tsx frontend/scripts/verify-listing-legacy-redirects.mjs; do printf '%s ' "$f"; awk '!/^[[:space:]]*$/ && !/^[[:space:]]*(\/\/|#|--)/' "$f" | wc -l; done
commands.result: layout.tsx: 7 pure LOC; verify-listing-legacy-redirects.mjs: 109 pure LOC.

commands.name: git diff whitespace check
commands.result: Passed with no stdout or stderr.

acceptance_checklist.observable: frontend/src/app/(app)/dashboard/listings/[id]/layout.tsx imports only ReactNode and returns children directly.

acceptance_checklist.criterion: Legacy overview redirects to canonical overview
Legacy knowledge redirects to canonical knowledge
Legacy logistics redirects to canonical logistics
Legacy offers redirects to canonical offers
Legacy SPA redirects to canonical documents
Old route tree contains no legacy dark/gold listing UI implementation
acceptance_checklist.observable: Final static scan and verifier found no banned legacy implementation tokens in the old route tree.

known_limitations: Live browser navigation was not used because the app routes are auth-protected and may redirect through auth/session state before route rendering.
Verification used static Next route module checks plus TypeScript compilation; this directly validates redirect() targets and absence of legacy UI in the compatibility route modules.

adversarial_notes.dirty_worktree: The repository was dirty before this task. Scoped old page redirect files were already modified and were verified but not rewritten. Other dirty files include BACKLOG.md, DESIGN.md, app/main.py, canonical listings files, listing components, tests, and OMO artifacts.
adversarial_notes.stale_state: Existing task-4 evidence still records earlier /dashboard/listings findings from before T10; it is treated as stale historical evidence, not current state.
adversarial_notes.misleading_success_output: The first redirect-target rg command was accidentally quoted with shell-evaluated backticks and printed zsh no such file or directory messages while still showing route matches; it was not used as final evidence. The final static scan uses single-quoted regex and passed.
adversarial_notes.auth_live_router_limitation: A live browser final-URL check could be misleading because auth middleware may intercept old protected routes. The added verifier records the Next-router-compatible old-to-canonical map from the route modules without auth side effects.
adversarial_notes.redirect_loop_browser_back_behavior: Old /dashboard/listings/[id] route modules redirect one-way to canonical /listings/[id] routes. The canonical checked scope has no redirect() or /dashboard/listings match back to the old dashboard listing routes, which avoids browser Back bouncing between duplicate UIs.
